stories.title: RBC Canadian Open Preview — Fitzpatrick Chases Fourth Win, Fleetwood the Favorite
stories.angle: RBC Canadian Open starts Thursday June 11 at TPC Toronto at Osprey Valley; Tommy Fleetwood leads odds at +1200, Matt Fitzpatrick at +1300 chasing his fourth PGA Tour win of 2026; field includes Collin Morikawa, Brooks Koepka, Tony Finau, Max Homa; $9.8M purse; Memorial champion JT Poston withdrew to rest

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
The RBC Canadian Open starts tomorrow at TPC Toronto.

Tommy Fleetwood: +1200 favorite.
Matt Fitzpatrick: +1300, chasing his 4th win of 2026.

$9.8M on the line. No Scheffler, no McIlroy. Wide-open field.

Who wins Canada? ⛳ #RBCCanadianOpen #PGATour

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Matt Fitzpatrick has 3 PGA Tour wins in 2026 and is eyeing a 4th this week in Toronto.

Tommy Fleetwood is the favorite after his first Tour win last August.

Neither Scheffler nor McIlroy is in the field.

This Canadian Open is anyone's to win. #RBCCanadianOpen

article.headline: RBC Canadian Open Preview: Fitzpatrick Chases History, Fleetwood Leads the Way at TPC Toronto

research.facts:
RBC Canadian Open Preview
**Verified facts:**
- Tournament: June 11-14, 2026, TPC Toronto at Osprey Valley (North Course), Caledon, Ontario — [HIGH]
- Purse: $9,800,000 — [HIGH]
- Tommy Fleetwood: +1200 odds (FanDuel favorite) — [HIGH]
- Matt Fitzpatrick: +1300 odds; chasing 4th PGA Tour win of 2026 — [HIGH]
- Field includes: Collin Morikawa, Brooks Koepka, Tony Finau, Max Homa, Jacob Bridgeman — [HIGH]
- JT Poston withdrew (WD) from the field — [HIGH]
- Fitzpatrick previous best at Canadian Open: T10 in 2022 — [HIGH]
- Fleetwood won FedExCup at 2025 Tour Championship (his first PGA Tour win) — [HIGH]
- Fleetwood's best at Canadian Open: runner-up 2023 (lost to Nick Taylor in 4-hole playoff) — [HIGH]
- No Scheffler or McIlroy in field — [HIGH]
**Sources:** RBC Canadian Open official site, Golf Channel, CBS Sports, Golf Digest

stories.title: LIV Golf's Survival Crisis — CEO Says "Trust PIF" But Can't Guarantee Final Four Events
stories.angle: LIV CEO Scott O'Neil told CNBC on June 9 that the league must take Saudi Arabia's PIF "at their word" to fund the rest of the 2026 season, but could not guarantee the final four tournaments would take place; PIF withdrawing after 2026; LIV seeking $250-$350M from investors; bankruptcy planning underway; new independent board established; PIF chairman stepped down

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
LIV Golf CEO Scott O'Neil this week:

— PIF is withdrawing funding after 2026
— Cannot guarantee final 4 tournaments will happen
— League seeking $250-350M from investors
— Bankruptcy plans in place

His advice: take PIF "at their word." ⛳ #LIVGolf

article.headline: LIV Golf's Survival Crisis: CEO Can't Guarantee Final Four Events as Bankruptcy Plans Take Shape

---

research.facts:
LIV Golf Survival Crisis
**Verified facts:**
- LIV CEO Scott O'Neil statement (June 9, CNBC): league must take PIF "at their word" to fund rest of season — [HIGH]
- O'Neil could not guarantee the final four tournaments will take place — [HIGH]
- PIF withdrawing funding at end of 2026 season — [HIGH]
- PIF invested more than $5 billion in LIV over its first years; expected to exceed $6B by end of 2026 — [HIGH]
- LIV seeking $250-$350M from investors — [HIGH]
- Bloomberg reported LIV is laying groundwork for US Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing — [HIGH]
- LIV planning to relocate headquarters to USA for potential bankruptcy — [HIGH]
- New independent board established: led by Gene Davis and Jon Zinman — [HIGH]
- PIF chairman Yasir Al-Rumayyan stepped down from LIV board — [HIGH]
- Investment banking advisor Ducera Partners engaged to find investors — [HIGH]
- O'Neil had 5 formal meetings about investor interest; 18 more planned for the week of June 9 — [HIGH]
**Sources:** CNBC, Yahoo Sports, ESPN, Golf Monthly, The Mirror US

stories.title: Scheffler's Grand Slam Date With Destiny — US Open on His 30th Birthday
stories.angle: US Open at Shinnecock Hills June 18-21; Scheffler has won 2 Masters, the PGA Championship, and The Open Championship — a US Open win would make him the 7th player in history to complete the career Grand Slam; final round June 21 is his 30th birthday; Scheffler's Shinnecock scouting: wide fairways (48 yards avg), brutally difficult green complexes; McIlroy (Masters champion) also in field

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Scottie Scheffler has won:

— 2x Masters
— The Open Championship
— PGA Championship

One major left for the career Grand Slam: the US Open.

It's at Shinnecock Hills June 18-21. The final round is on his 30th birthday. ⛳ #USOpen

article.headline: Scheffler at Shinnecock: The Grand Slam, the 30th Birthday, and the Biggest Stage in Golf

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Scheffler's Grand Slam Bid

#### Image Concept 1 — X Social (1080x1350)
- **Type:** imagn photo + text overlay
- **Search terms:** "Scottie Scheffler PGA Tour"
- **Overlay text:** "THE CAREER GRAND SLAM. JUNE 21. HIS 30TH BIRTHDAY."
- **Design notes:** Historic milestone framing, blue/white US Open palette

#### Image Concept 2 — Facebook (1080x1350)
- **Type:** imagn photo + text overlay
- **Search terms:** "Scottie Scheffler PGA Tour"

- **Design notes:** Checklist format, anticipation/build-up energy

---

research.facts:
Scheffler's Grand Slam Bid at Shinnecock
**Verified facts:**
- US Open: June 18-21, 2026, Shinnecock Hills Golf Club, Southampton, NY — [HIGH]
- Final round June 21 = Scheffler's 30th birthday — [HIGH]
- Scheffler wins needed for Grand Slam: Masters (x2 career), PGA Championship, The Open Championship (all won); needs US Open — [HIGH]
- A US Open win would make Scheffler the 7th career Grand Slam player in history — [HIGH]
- Shinnecock: fairways avg 48 yards wide (widest setup in 50-75 years per reports) — [HIGH]
- Scheffler on course: green complexes are "extremely difficult" and the greatest challenge — [HIGH]
- This is the 126th US Open; Shinnecock's 6th time hosting the major — [HIGH]
- Last time Shinnecock hosted (2018): Brooks Koepka won at 1-over par — [HIGH]
- Rory McIlroy (2026 Masters champion) is also in the field — [HIGH]
**Sources:** ESPN, FanSided, Washington Post, NBC Sports, Golf Digest

stories.title: JT Poston Skips Canada — Why the Memorial Winner Needed a Break
stories.angle: Poston withdrew from the RBC Canadian Open after his exhausting Memorial Tournament victory — he played 33 holes on Sunday in brutal conditions, beat Ryan Gerard in a 2-hole playoff, earned $4M and his 4th PGA Tour win, and secured a US Open exemption; his withdrawal is justified but opens the door for others at TPC Toronto

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
JT Poston has withdrawn from the RBC Canadian Open.

Understandable. He played 33 holes last Sunday at the Memorial, beat Ryan Gerard in a 2-hole playoff, won $4M, and earned a US Open exemption.

You earn the rest, JT. 🏆 #PGATour

article.headline: Why JT Poston's Canadian Open Withdrawal Is Completely Justified

research.facts:
JT Poston WD From Canadian Open
**Verified facts:**
- JT Poston withdrew from RBC Canadian Open — [HIGH]
- Reason: recovering from exhausting Memorial Tournament victory — [HIGH]
- Memorial: Poston played 33 holes on final Sunday (weather delay) — [HIGH]
- Memorial: beat Ryan Gerard in 2-hole playoff — [HIGH]
- Memorial win: $4M, 4th PGA Tour career win, US Open exemption — [HIGH]
- World ranking improved from 94th to 39th after Memorial win — [HIGH]
**Sources:** Golf Monthly (field level media), Field Level Media

stories.title: Dow LPGA International Preview — Team Golf Returns at Midland This Week
stories.angle: The Dow Championship (LPGA Tour, June 11-14) returns to Midland, Michigan with 72 two-player teams competing in an alternate-shot/better-ball format; $3.3M purse; one of the most unique events on the LPGA calendar

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
The Dow Championship tees off Thursday in Midland, Michigan.

72 two-player LPGA teams. $3.3M purse. One of the most unique formats in professional golf.

While the men play Toronto, the women take center stage in Michigan. ⛳ #LPGA #DowChampionship

article.headline: Dow Championship Preview: Why Team Golf Brings Out the Best in the LPGA Tour
